name: aibrix

metadata.name: aibrix
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: AIBrix, the foundational building blocks for constructing your own GenAI inference infrastructure.

documentation:
# AI Runtime
A versatile sidecar enabling metric standardization, model downloading, and management.

## Quick Start
### Installation
AI Runtime can be installed by `pip`.

```sh
pip install aibrix
```

### Model download
The AI Runtime supports model downloading from the following storage backends:
* HuggingFace
* S3
* TOS

For more details on model downloading, please refer to our [Runtime docs](https://github.com/aibrix/aibrix/blob/main/docs/source/features/runtime.rst#model-downloading).

### Integrate with inference engines
The AI Runtime hides various implementation details on the inference engine side, providing a universal method to guide model management, as well as expose inference monitoring metrics.

At present, `vLLM` engine is supported, and in the future, `SGLang` and other inference engines will be supported.

For more details on integrate with `vLLM`, please refer to our [Runtime docs](https://github.com/aibrix/aibrix/blob/main/docs/source/features/runtime.rst#metric-standardization).

## Contributing
We welcome contributions from the community! Check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/aibrix/aibrix/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) to see how you can make a difference.

### Build from source

```bash
# This may take several minutes
pip install -e .
```

### Lint, Format and Type Check

Before contribute your code, please run the following commands to ensure that your code passes the tests and linting checks.

```bash
# install dependencies
poetry install --no-root --with dev

# linting, formatting and type checking
bash ./scripts/format.sh
```

## License

AI Runtime is licensed under the [APACHE License](https://github.com/aibrix/aibrix/LICENSE.md).

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.initialize_storage
documentation:
Initialize job storage with storage type.

Args:
    storage_type: the storage type, such files or TOS

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.submit_job_input
documentation:
Upload job input data file to storage.

Args:
    inputDataFile (str): an input file string. Each line is a request.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.delete_job
documentation:
Delete job given job ID. This removes all data associated this job,
including input data and output results.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.get_job_input_requests
documentation:
Read job input requests specified job Id.

Args:
    job_id : job_id is returned by job submission.
    start_index : request starting index for read.
    num_requests: total number of requests needed to read.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.get_job_num_request
documentation: Get the number of valid requests for this submitted job.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.put_job_results
documentation:
Write job requests results to storage.

Args:
    job_id : job_id is returned by job submission.
    start_index : requests index to write.
    requests_results: a list of json objects as request results to write.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.get_job_results
documentation:
Read job requests results from storage.

Args:
    job_id : job_id is returned by job submission.
    start_index : requests index to read.
    num_requests: total number of requests output needed to read.

classes.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.PersistentStorage
documentation:
This is an abstract class.

A storage should implement this class, such as Local files, TOS and S3.
Any storage implementation are transparent to external components.

classes.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.write_job_input_data
documentation: This writes requests file to local disk.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.read_job_input_data
documentation: Read job requests input from local disk.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.get_job_number_requests
documentation: Get job requests length from local disk.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.write_job_output_data
documentation: Write job results output as files.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.read_job_output_data
documentation: Read job results output from local disk as files.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.generic_storage.LocalDiskFiles.delete_job_data
documentation: Delete all input and output files for the job.
This stores all job data in local disk as files.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.__init__
documentation:
This initializes client configuration to a
TOS online bucket. All job information will be submitted to this
TOS bucket. The two input keys are from Volcano TOS account.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.write_job_input_data
documentation:
Each request is an object.
the format is jobID_input_requestId

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.read_job_input_data
documentation: Read job input request data

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.write_job_output_data
documentation:
Write job results to TOS bucket.
The key of job request output is in the format of
jobID_output_requestId.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.read_job_output_data
documentation: Read job results output from TOS bucket.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.delete_job_data
documentation:
This deletes all request data for the given job ID,
including both input data and output data.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.storage.tos_storage.TOSStorage.get_job_number_requests
documentation: This read the number of reqeust by listing the number of input requests.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.initialize_batch_storage
documentation:
Initialize storage type. Now it support files and TOS.

For some storage type, user needs to pass in other parameters to params.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.upload_input_data
documentation:
Upload job input data file to storage.

Args:
    inputDataFileName (str): an input file string.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.read_job_requests
documentation:
Read job requests starting at index: start_index.

Instead of reading from storage per request, this maintains a list of requests
in memory with a length of NUM_REQUESTS_PER_READ.
It also supports random access, if backward read is necessary.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.put_storage_job_results
documentation: Write job results on a specific index.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.get_storage_job_results
documentation: Read job requests results.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.remove_storage_job_data
documentation: Remove job all relevant data.

functions.name: aibrix.batch.storage.batch_storage.get_job_request_len
documentation: Get the number of requests for the job_id.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.driver.BatchDriver.__init__
documentation: This is main entrance to bind all components to serve job requests.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.driver.BatchDriver.jobs_running_loop
documentation:
This loop is going through all active jobs in scheduler.
For now, the executing unit is one request. Later if necessary,
we can support a batch size of request per execution.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.request_proxy.RequestProxy.execute_queries
documentation:
This is the entrance to inference engine.
This fetches request input from storage and submit request
to inference engine. Lastly the result is stored back to storage.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.request_proxy.RequestProxy.fetch_request_input
documentation:
Read request input from storage. Now it only reads one request.
Later we can add a list as a batch per call.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.request_proxy.RequestProxy.store_output
documentation: Write the request result back to storage.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.request_proxy.RequestProxy.sync_job_status
documentation: Update job's status back to job manager.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.request_proxy.InferenceEngineClient.__init__
documentation:
Initiate client to inference engine, such as account
and its authentication.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.scheduler.BasicCongestionControl.tighten_jobs
documentation:
We are intentional to move all current in-progress jobs
to the front part of the pool.
Two advantages of doing this:
1. it facilitates the following resource adjustment by update the tail of the pool.
2. it guarantees the jobs' order if necessary.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.scheduler.BasicCongestionControl.update_job_pool_size
documentation:
This is the call exposed to external components.
[TODO] Spawn a new process to monitor resource and apply
necessary adjustment from here.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.scheduler.JobScheduler.__init__
documentation:
self._jobs_queue are all the jobs.
self._due_jobs_list stores all potential jobs that can be marked
as expired jobs.
self._inactive_jobs are jobs that are already invalid.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.scheduler.JobScheduler.job_cleanup_loop
documentation: This is a long-running process to check if jobs have expired or not.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobMetaInfo.__init__
documentation:
This constructs a full set of metadata for a batch job.
Later if needed, this can include other extral metadata
as an easy extention.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobMetaInfo.complete_one_request
documentation:
This is called after an inference call. If all requests
are done, we need to update its status to be completed.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobMetaInfo.next_request_id
documentation:
Returns the next request for inference. Due to the propobility
that some requests are failed, this returns a request that
are not marked as executed.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobMetaInfo.validate_job
documentation:
This handles all validations before successfully creating a job.
This is also the place connecting other components
to check connection and status.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.__init__
documentation:
This manages jobs in three categorical job pools.
1. _pending_jobs are jobs that are not scheduled yet
2. _in_progress_jobs are jobs that are in progress now.
Theses are the input to the job scheduler.
3. _done_jobs are inactive jobs. This needs to be updated periodically.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.create_job
documentation:
This interface is exposed to users to submit a new job and create
a job accordingly.
Before calling this, user needs to submit job input to storage first
to have job ID ready.
This will validate a job with multiple checking steps.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.cancel_job
documentation:
This is called by user to cancel a created job.
if this job is pending or done, we directly remove it.
if the job is in progress, we also need to remove the requests
in model proxy.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.get_job_status
documentation:
This retrieves a job's status to users.
Job scheduler does not need to check job status. It can directly
check the job pool for scheduling, such as pending_jobs.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.start_execute_job
documentation:
This interface should be called by scheduler.
User is not allowed to choose a job to be scheduled.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.mark_job_progress
documentation:
This is used to sync job's progress, called by execution proxy.
It is guaranteed that each request is executed at least once.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.expire_job
documentation:
This is called by scheduler. When a job arrives at its
specified due time, scheduler will mark this expired.
User can not expire a job, but can cancel a job.

methods.name: aibrix.batch.job_manager.JobManager.sync_job_to_storage
documentation:
[TODO] Xin
This is used to serialize everything here to storage to make sure
that job manager can restart it over from storage once it crashes
or intentional quit.
